methodsAttempted.query: site:ntua.gr National Technical University of Athens generative AI ChatGPT policy artificial intelligence students teaching
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search did not surface a central NTUA generative-AI policy; it surfaced one official NTUA AI education news page plus unrelated non-NTUA results.

methodsAttempted.query: site:ntua.gr ChatGPT ΕΜΠ τεχνητή νοημοσύνη παραγωγική τεχνητή νοημοσύνη
methodsAttempted.notes: Greek-language official-domain searches found a public Helios course exam PDF that explicitly mentions LLMs, plus official NTUA news/research items rather than a broad university AI-use policy.
The www.ntua.gr robots.txt disallows administrative and Joomla system paths but not the public news item paths used for verified sources.

methodsAttempted.query: helios.ntua.gr ChatGPT Gemini Claude ΕΜΠ pdf
methodsAttempted.notes: The public Helios resource redirected to an official NTUA Moodle-hosted PDF for a Civil Engineering course exam announcement. helios.ntua.gr/robots.txt returned 404, so no robots disallow was found.

artifacts.summary: Official NTUA discovery found no reliable public university-wide generative-AI use policy. Verified extractable evidence is limited to a public Helios course exam PDF with an explicit LLM prohibition for that exam and an official NTUA news page about a new AI course. A separate official Mistral AI talent-program news item was documented but rejected for policy extraction because it is a program announcement rather than policy or guidance.

artifacts.claimText: For the NTUA Civil Engineering course 'Σχεδίαση Τεχνικών Έργων με Η/Υ' in-person exam scheduled for February 2, 2026, the course announcement strictly prohibits use of large language models such as ChatGPT, Gemini, and Claude during the exam.

artifacts.title: National Technical University of Athens AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: The crawl did not locate a reliable public NTUA-wide generative-AI use policy. Staged candidates are limited to a Civil Engineering course exam PDF that strictly prohibits LLM use during that specific exam and an official NTUA news page stating that a new AI course covers ethical issues and social impacts. An official Mistral AI talent-program news item was rejected for claim extraction because it is news/program material rather than policy guidance.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
No central public NTUA-wide generative-AI policy was found in this crawl; claims are intentionally scoped to the source text.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
